level_signals: 5+ years of technical program management experience required
First dedicated TPM for Data Science at Figma — high-ownership, scoped program lead
Base salary range $185K–$317K USD consistent with Senior IC at a major tech company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of technical program management experience (or equivalent) driving data infrastructure, analytics platform, or data democratization programs
must_haves.qualifier: data infrastructure, analytics platform, or data democratization programs

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Hands-on experience with modern data stacks: you're comfortable discussing semantic layers, data catalogs, BI tooling (Hex, Sigma), and AI-powered query interfaces (Cortex Analyst, RAG) at a program level
A track record of driving adoption — not just shipping internal tools, but changing behavior across technical and non-technical audiences through change management, enablement, and champion programs
Strong cross-functional program delivery: planning, dependency management, stakeholder alignment across deeply distributed teams

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Hands-on familiarity with dbt, Snowflake Semantic Views, Acryl, Dagster, or similar data engineering tooling
Experience building or scaling eval frameworks for AI-generated outputs (golden SQL, accuracy dashboards, regression detection)
Background in data-driven product analytics or developer tools — understanding of how DS ICs work with PMs and EMs and driving alignment on requirements
Strong bias to action, self-motivation and curiosity, with a desire to bring people together in a fast-paced environment

scope.surface: Self-Serve Analytics program for Figma's Data Science org — semantic layer expansion, Slack bot, automated eval framework, Figma Data Assistant
primary_metrics_inferred: % of data questions answered via self-serve
core_responsibilities: Own end-to-end program delivery for Figma's Self-Serve Analytics initiative
Coordinate delivery across Data Engineering, DS ICs, Security, IT, and business stakeholders
Lead governance and security work stream including LLM data access policies, PII guardrails, access controls, and LLM provider approvals
Drive adoption and change management through champion networks, training programs, and enablement
Build and maintain DS org operations: planning cycles, headcount tracking, onboarding programs, and team rhythms
Coordinate with peer TPMs and Data Scientists on overlapping platform programs and shared data infrastructure

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and wide ($185K–$317K base) for flexibility across seniority
First dedicated TPM for Data Science — high visibility, greenfield ownership
Explicitly encourages applicants whose experience doesn't perfectly match the JD
Remote-US eligible in addition to SF and NYC hubs
Clear, concrete program scope (Self-Serve Analytics initiative) with named deliverables
red_flags: Very wide base salary range ($185K–$317K) makes leveling and actual offer difficult to predict
Remote pay is localized at 80–100% of hub range — remote candidates may receive significantly less
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned anywhere in JD
In-person onboarding required even for remote hires — travel burden not quantified

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and greenfield scope; not explicitly stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from JD responsibilities text — not framed as 'metrics' per se)
equity_form (equity mentioned but form not specified)
ambiguities: Role is described as 'can be held from one of our US hubs or remotely in the United States' — treated as hybrid for hub-based employees and remote-us for others; location.type set to hybrid as hub presence is implied for SF/NY candidates
Pay range $185K–$317K is very wide; could span multiple levels; seniority inferred as Senior IC based on years requirement and scope but not confirmed
The JD lists Slack and Notion as platforms where the TPM will meet Figmates — unclear if these are required tool proficiencies or just environmental context; not elevated to must-haves
